response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced software developer with a broad background in web, embedded, and distributed systems, but he is critically misaligned with the core requirements of this role. The minimum qualification demands 8 years of C++ experience, yet C++ does not appear in his listed skills and his only traceable C++ work is a 7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor's thesis project — roughly 1 year total. His technical depth lives in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Similarly, the role requires 5 years building large-scale cloud/distributed infrastructure, and while he has touched Kubernetes, AWS CDK, and high-throughput micro-services, none of these engagements reflect sustained, production-level cloud platform ownership at the required depth. His software craftsmanship, TDD discipline, and mentoring activity are genuine strengths, but they do not offset the fundamental skill-stack mismatch for a C++-centric cloud compute platform lead.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship culture: consistent TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and pairing practices across multiple roles
Breadth of systems experience spanning embedded, high-throughput micro-services, mobile, and infrastructure tooling (Docker, Kubernetes, GitLab CI)
Demonstrated mentoring and knowledge-sharing through workshops, conference talks, and internal Refactoring Dojos/Code Kata sessions
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~7 months at NI + one thesis project), far below the 8-year minimum qualification — the single most disqualifying gap
No evidence of sustained, large-scale cloud infrastructure ownership; AWS CDK and Kubernetes usage appears auxiliary rather than platform-level

No formal technical lead or engineering management tenure; 'Staff' titles at re:cinq and Altom reflect IC seniority, not the cross-team leadership, roadmap ownership, and architectural governance this role requires
